instructions: Copy this file into workspace/STATUS.json before tracking workflow state for a new project.
Use this as the default end-to-end workflow scaffold for the repo, then adapt item wording only if the project truly needs a different process.
Keep each item as a durable milestone that proves a workflow step is established, not a promise that all future iteration is finished.
Keep sourceFiles aligned with the workspace files that prove each item is complete.

items.text: Current phase is confirmed and up to date in PROJECT.json.
STATUS.json reflects the next concrete workflow step.
IDEA.md captures the irreducible concept, core meaning, and core sequence.
PROJECT.json defines the project metadata, target runtime, audience, emotional effect, phase, and active model choices.
PROJECT.md is updated as a readable summary of the current canonical project state.
STORY.md locks the logline, synopsis, arc, themes, and scene goals.
CHARACTERS.md defines recurring character canon and prompt-safe identity anchors.
STYLE.md locks the visual language, palette, lighting, camera behavior, and forbidden drift.
CONTINUITY.md records the canon checkpoints, dependencies, risks, and mitigation notes that must survive revisions.
SOUND.md defines the sound arc, sonic continuity, music guidance, and forbidden drift.
MODELS.md captures the model-specific guidance needed for the active image and video workflow.
REFERENCES.json defines the collection targets needed to guide look, subject, environment, or shot planning.
REFERENCES.json tracks the real supplied, candidate, approved, or rejected assets relevant to the project.
STORYBOARD.json defines the scene and shot plan with durations, transitions, continuity dependencies, and frame states.
STORYBOARD.md is updated as a readable summary of the canonical shot plan.
KEYFRAMES.json defines the active image model, reusable anchors, and workflow notes for still-image development.
KEYFRAMES.json contains per-shot frame goals and still-image prompts for the key moments that need visual proof.
Approved keyframes are reflected back into REFERENCES.json and any canon files that changed because the images established new project truth.
PROMPT-PACK.json contains executable per-shot video prompts, model choice, and keyframe links where required.
TESTS.json records the targeted experiments or acceptance checks needed to de-risk the workflow.
GENERATION-LOG.jsonl contains the first real generation records tied to prompts, shots, and outputs.
QC.json defines stable review categories for evaluating generations consistently.
QC.json contains real shot-level review entries tied to generation IDs from GENERATION-LOG.jsonl.
EDIT.json tracks the intended cut order and can bind approved generations to planned shots.
PROJECT.json and STATUS.json are resynced after the latest review cycle or phase change.
